 {"id":"caam-0eq","title":"Health status display in CLI","description":"# Health Status Display in CLI

## Purpose
Show profile health status indicators in CLI commands like `caam list` and `caam status`.

## User Experience Goals
Users should see at a glance:
- Which profiles are healthy
- Which profiles have issues
- What the issues are (expiring, errors, etc.)

## Display Formats

### `caam list` Output
```
$ caam list
Claude Profiles
  ● work@company.com      🟢 Pro    59m left   [ACTIVE]
    personal@gmail.com    🟡 Free   12m left   
    test@example.com      🔴 Expired            

Codex Profiles
  ● main                  🟢        23h left   [ACTIVE]
    backup                ⚪ Unknown            

Gemini Profiles
    default               🟢        Valid
```

### `caam status` Output
```
$ caam status
Active Profiles
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
claude    work@company.com    🟢 Healthy    59m left
codex     main                🟢 Healthy    23h left
gemini    (none)              

Warnings
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
claude/personal@gmail.com: Token expires in 12 minutes
claude/test@example.com: Token expired

Recommendations
───────────────────────────────────────────────────
• Run "caam refresh claude personal@gmail.com" to refresh expiring token
• Run "caam login claude test@example.com" to re-authenticate
```

### Status Column Format
```
🟢 59m left     - Healthy with time remaining
🟡 12m left     - Warning, expiring soon
🔴 Expired      - Critical, needs attention
🔴 3 errors     - Critical, multiple recent errors
⚪ Unknown      - Cannot determine status
```

## Technical Requirements

### Integration Points
```go
// In list command
profiles := store.List(provider)
for _, p := range profiles {
    health := healthStore.GetProfile(provider, p.Name)
    status, _ := health.CalculateHealth()
    fmt.Printf("  %s %s %s\n", 
        activeIndicator(p),
        p.Name,
        formatHealthStatus(status, health))
}
```

### Helper Functions
```go
func formatHealthStatus(status HealthStatus, h *ProfileHealth) string
func formatTimeRemaining(expiry time.Time) string
func formatStatusWithReason(status HealthStatus, h *ProfileHealth) string
```

## Color Support
Use lipgloss styles consistent with TUI:
- 🟢 Green for healthy
- 🟡 Yellow for warning
- 🔴 Red for critical
- Gray for unknown

Support `--no-color` flag for piping.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `caam list` shows health status for each profile
- [ ] `caam status` shows active profiles with health
- [ ] `caam status` shows warnings and recommendations
- [ ] Time remaining formatted naturally (59m, 23h, 3d)
- [ ] Colors disabled with --no-color or non-TTY
- [ ] Unit tests for formatting functions

## Files to Create/Modify
- `cmd/caam/cmd/list.go` (modify)
- `cmd/caam/cmd/status.go` (modify)
- `internal/health/format.go` (new)
- `internal/health/format_test.go` (new)

## Dependencies
- Health status calculation (caam-3ef)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:18:49.689326323-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:26:54.046325238-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:26:54.046325238-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented health status display in CLI. Updated status and ls commands to show detailed health info using HealthStore with fallback to parsing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-0eq","depends_on_id":"caam-3ef","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:37.521450734-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-0gb","title":"Smart Profile Management configuration","description":"## Purpose
Create unified configuration for Smart Profile Management features.

## Background
Multiple features need configuration:
- Refresh threshold (default 10 minutes)
- Health status thresholds
- Penalty decay rate
- Data retention days
- File watching enabled/disabled

Rather than scatter these settings, create a unified config system.

## Configuration Location
~/.caam/config.yaml

## Schema
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
version: 1

# Health & Refresh settings
health:
  refresh_threshold: 10m        # Refresh tokens expiring within this time
  warning_threshold: 1h         # Yellow status below this TTL
  penalty_decay_rate: 0.8       # 20% decay every 5 minutes
  penalty_decay_interval: 5m
  
# Analytics settings
analytics:
  enabled: true
  retention_days: 90            # Keep detailed logs
  aggregate_retention_days: 365
  cleanup_on_startup: true
  
# Runtime settings
runtime:
  file_watching: true
  reload_on_sighup: true
  pid_file: true

# Project associations
project:
  enabled: true
  auto_activate: false          # Auto-activate based on CWD
```

## Technical Implementation

### Config Package
```go
// internal/config/config.go
type Config struct {
    Version   int            `yaml:"version"`
    Health    HealthConfig   `yaml:"health"`
    Analytics AnalyticsConfig `yaml:"analytics"`
    Runtime   RuntimeConfig  `yaml:"runtime"`
    Project   ProjectConfig  `yaml:"project"`
}

type HealthConfig struct {
    RefreshThreshold     time.Duration `yaml:"refresh_threshold"`
    WarningThreshold     time.Duration `yaml:"warning_threshold"`
    PenaltyDecayRate     float64       `yaml:"penalty_decay_rate"`
    PenaltyDecayInterval time.Duration `yaml:"penalty_decay_interval"`
}

// DefaultConfig returns sensible defaults
func DefaultConfig() *Config

// Load reads config from file, falling back to defaults
func Load() (*Config, error)

// Save writes config to file
func (c *Config) Save() error
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# View current config
caam config show

# Set a value
caam config set health.refresh_threshold 5m

# Reset to defaults
caam config reset
```

## Files to Create
- internal/config/config.go
- internal/config/config_test.go
- cmd/caam/config.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config file parsed from ~/.caam/config.yaml
- [ ] Sensible defaults when file missing
- [ ] CLI commands for viewing and editing
- [ ] Duration parsing works (10m, 1h, etc.)
- [ ] Unknown fields ignored (forward compatible)
- [ ] Validation of values (no negative durations)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:31:42.196043884-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:13:15.110015636-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:13:15.110015636-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SPM configuration with YAML support, CLI commands, and tests"}
 {"id":"caam-0gx","title":"File system watcher for profile changes","description":"## Purpose
Implement file system watching to detect profile changes in real-time using fsnotify.

## Background
codex-pool reloads credentials without restart:
```go
POST /admin/reload → h.pool.replace(newAccounts)
```

For caam, we watch the profiles directory and notify when files change.
This enables the TUI to auto-refresh without manual intervention.

## Watched Paths
```
~/.caam/profiles/        # Profile storage directory
~/.caam/profiles/claude/ # Provider subdirectories
~/.caam/profiles/codex/
~/.caam/profiles/gemini/
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// internal/watcher/watcher.go
package watcher

import (
    "github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"
)

// Event types
type EventType int

const (
    EventProfileAdded EventType = iota
    EventProfileModified
    EventProfileDeleted
)

// Event represents a file system change
type Event struct {
    Type     EventType
    Provider string
    Profile  string
    Path     string
}

// Watcher monitors profile directories for changes
type Watcher struct {
    fsWatcher *fsnotify.Watcher
    events    chan Event
    errors    chan error
    done      chan struct{}
}

// New creates a new profile watcher
func New(profilesDir string) (*Watcher, error) {
    fsw, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    w := &Watcher{
        fsWatcher: fsw,
        events:    make(chan Event, 100),
        errors:    make(chan error, 10),
        done:      make(chan struct{}),
    }
    
    // Watch base directory and all provider subdirs
    if err := fsw.Add(profilesDir); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // Watch each provider directory
    for _, provider := range []string{"claude", "codex", "gemini"} {
        providerDir := filepath.Join(profilesDir, provider)
        if _, err := os.Stat(providerDir); err == nil {
            fsw.Add(providerDir)
        }
    }
    
    go w.run()
    return w, nil
}

func (w *Watcher) run() {
    for {
        select {
        case event := <-w.fsWatcher.Events:
            if e := w.translateEvent(event); e != nil {
                w.events <- *e
            }
        case err := <-w.fsWatcher.Errors:
            w.errors <- err
        case <-w.done:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (w *Watcher) translateEvent(e fsnotify.Event) *Event {
    // Parse path to extract provider and profile name
    // Filter out non-profile files (e.g., .DS_Store)
    // Debounce rapid successive events
}

func (w *Watcher) Events() <-chan Event { return w.events }
func (w *Watcher) Errors() <-chan error { return w.errors }
func (w *Watcher) Close() error { close(w.done); return w.fsWatcher.Close() }
```

## Event Debouncing
File saves often trigger multiple events. Debounce to avoid redundant refreshes:
```go
type debouncer struct {
    pending map[string]time.Time
    delay   time.Duration
}

func (d *debouncer) shouldEmit(path string) bool {
    if last, ok := d.pending[path]; ok {
        if time.Since(last) < d.delay {
            return false
        }
    }
    d.pending[path] = time.Now()
    return true
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/watcher/watcher.go
- internal/watcher/watcher_test.go
- internal/watcher/debounce.go

## Dependencies
- github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects profile additions, modifications, deletions
- [ ] Correctly identifies provider and profile from path
- [ ] Debounces rapid events (configurable delay, default 100ms)
- [ ] Handles directory creation (new provider added)
- [ ] Clean shutdown without goroutine leaks
- [ ] Ignores non-profile files (.DS_Store, .git, etc.)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:24:02.784501695-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T19:25:37.771554993-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:25:37.771554993-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented fsnotify-based profile watcher with debounced events, recursive dir watching (no symlinks), provider/profile parsing, and tests.","comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"caam-0gx","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting implementation of internal/watcher using fsnotify to emit debounced profile add/modify/delete events for ~/.local/share/caam/profiles/<provider>/<name>/... . Will include translate logic + unit tests.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:11:00Z"},{"id":12,"issue_id":"caam-0gx","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented internal/watcher using fsnotify: recursive directory watching (no symlinks), profile-level event translation (provider/profile from path), debounced modified events (default 100ms), provider-dir bootstrap scan so first-run provider+profile creation emits ProfileAdded reliably. Added fsnotify dependency + unit/integration tests. (Agent mail not configured in this env; using issue comments for coordination.)","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:25:24Z"}]}
-{"id":"caam-0gy","title":"Session resume support for Codex profiles","description":"# Session Resume for Profile Isolation Mode

## IMPORTANT: Primary Workflow Already Works!

For the main "hit limit → switch → resume" workflow, **no new caam command is needed**:

\`\`\`bash
# Using Auth File Swapping mode (PRIMARY)
codex                              # Uses ~/.codex/
# ... hit limit, get session ID ...
caam activate codex backup         # Swaps auth file only
codex resume <session_id> "proceed"  # WORKS - sessions still in ~/.codex/
\`\`\`

Sessions stay in \`~/.codex/sessions/\` - only the auth file changes. Native \`codex resume\` works perfectly.

## When This Task Would Be Useful

This task is only needed for **Profile Isolation mode** (\`caam exec\`), where:
- Each profile has its own CODEX_HOME
- Sessions are stored per-profile, not shared
- Cross-profile resume wouldn't work without this

## Deprioritized

Since the primary workflow already works, this is P3 (nice-to-have for advanced users using profile isolation).

## If Implemented

\`\`\`bash
# For profile isolation mode only
caam resume codex work              # Resume last session for isolated profile
caam resume codex work --last       # Same, explicit
caam resume codex work "proceed"    # With prompt
\`\`\`

Would set CODEX_HOME to profile's directory, then run \`codex resume --last\`.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T20:12:30.022248297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:32:48.123529249-05:00","comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"caam-0gy","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting: implement codex session ID capture in `caam exec` + new `caam resume codex <profile>` command w/ stored last session ID in profile metadata (profile.json). Agent mail not configured; coordinating via bd comments.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:23:45Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-1ba","title":"Additional CLI Commands","description":"## Overview
Polish commands that improve the CLI experience for power users.

## Commands to Add
1. `init` - Initialize caam, create directories, guided setup
2. `which` - Show current default profile for each provider
3. `use` - Set default profile for a provider
4. `env` - Print environment variables for shell eval
5. `open` - Open provider account page in browser
6. `doctor` - Diagnose setup issues
7. `logout` - Logout from isolated profile (complement to existing `clear`)

## Priority
These are nice-to-have polish features. Core functionality works without them.

## User Value
- `use`/`which`: Less typing for frequent users
- `env`: Shell integration for advanced workflows
- `doctor`: Self-service troubleshooting
- `open`: Quick access to account management","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:55.263150841-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:40:41.684230005-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:40:41.684230005-05:00","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: init, use/which, env, open, doctor. The logout functionality is already available via existing commands."}
-{"id":"caam-1r6","title":"Document headless authentication in AGENTS.md and README","description":"# Document Headless Workflows (HIGH PRIORITY)

## Key Insight: Most Workflows Already Work!

The main headless workflows work TODAY with existing commands. We just need to document them clearly.

## Documentation to Add

### AGENTS.md Section: Headless Server Workflows

\`\`\`markdown
### Headless Server Setup

#### Quick Setup (Auth File Swapping)

The simplest approach uses native CLI commands + caam backup:

\`\`\`bash
# On headless server - setup accounts (one-time)
codex login --device-auth        # Native Codex device code flow
caam backup codex account1       # Save to caam vault

codex logout
codex login --device-auth        # Login next account
caam backup codex account2       # Save it too
\`\`\`

#### Daily Workflow: Hit Limit → Switch → Resume

\`\`\`bash
codex                                    # Start working
# ... hit usage limit ...
# Codex shows: "To continue, run codex resume 019b2e3d-..."

caam activate codex account2             # Switch (< 1 second!)
codex resume 019b2e3d-... "proceed"      # Continue with new account!
\`\`\`

The session is preserved because sessions are in ~/.codex/sessions/ (unchanged).
Only the auth file (~/.codex/auth.json) is swapped.

#### Transfer Vault Between Machines

To copy your accounts to a new server:

\`\`\`bash
# On source machine
caam export --all > vault.tar.gz

# Transfer
scp vault.tar.gz user@newserver:~

# On new server
caam import vault.tar.gz
caam activate codex account1    # Ready to use!
\`\`\`
\`\`\`

### Key Points to Document

1. **Native device-code flow** - \`codex login --device-auth\` already works
2. **Session persistence across account switches** - sessions stay in ~/.codex/
3. **Export/import for vault transfer** - the one new feature needed
4. **Profile Isolation is advanced** - only needed for parallel sessions

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AGENTS.md has "Headless Server Workflows" section
- [ ] Documents native device-auth + caam backup workflow
- [ ] Documents hit-limit → switch → resume workflow
- [ ] Documents vault transfer workflow
- [ ] Clarifies that Profile Isolation is advanced/optional

## Dependencies

- caam-zha: Export/import commands (for vault transfer section)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:39:46.32179997-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:30:24.945478328-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-1r6","depends_on_id":"caam-ela","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:40:28.287670099-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-1r6","depends_on_id":"caam-zha","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T18:07:30.245096093-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"caam-1r6","author":"ubuntu","text":"caam-zha is now implemented/closed: `caam export`/`caam import` commands exist (tar.gz, manifest+sha256, atomic import, --force/--as). Headless docs can now recommend vault transfer as primary workflow.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:43:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-1xv","title":"Research and implement Claude setup-token for headless auth","description":"# Research and Implement Claude Headless Auth

## Research Findings (Completed)

### Available Methods

#### 1. setup-token + CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (Primary)
```bash
# Generate token
claude setup-token
# Returns: your-oauth-token-here

# Use in headless environment  
export CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN="your-oauth-token-here"
claude -p "your prompt"
```

**Known Issue**: GitHub #8938 reports the token doesn't fully bypass the startup interactive flow. User still sees theme/auth prompts even with token set.

#### 2. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (API Key Mode)
```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."
claude -p "your prompt"
```
Works for print mode (-p), may not work for interactive sessions.

#### 3. Credential File Transfer (Workaround)
Transfer ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json to remote machine:
```bash
scp ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json user@server:~/.config/claude-code/auth.json
```

#### 4. SSH Port Forwarding (Workaround)
```bash
# Local machine
ssh -L 8080:localhost:8080 user@remote-server
# On remote, run claude /login, open the localhost URL locally
```

### Current Status in Claude Code

- No native device code flow (like codex --device-auth)
- setup-token exists but has bugs (#8938)
- Credential transfer is the most reliable workaround
- GitHub #7100 requests proper headless auth documentation

### Implementation Path for caam

1. **AuthModeSetupToken**: New auth mode using CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
2. **Token Import**: Command to import setup-token result
3. **Credential Export/Import**: Enhanced backup/restore for headless transfer

### Files to Modify

- internal/provider/claude/claude.go
- Add setupToken flow that:
  a. Checks for CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var
  b. If not set, prompts user to run \`claude setup-token\` elsewhere
  c. Stores token in profile for future use

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Document current limitations clearly
- [ ] Implement token-based auth if feasible
- [ ] Fallback: Provide clear instructions for credential transfer via caam backup/restore

### References

- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/8938
- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/7100
- https://claude-did-this.com/claude-hub/getting-started/setup-container-guide","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:37:22.089377565-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:10.248176035-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:10.248176035-05:00","close_reason":"Research complete. Claude doesn't have reliable device-code. setup-token has bugs (GitHub #8938). Recommendation: Use vault transfer approach (caam export/import) which works universally."}
 {"id":"caam-234","title":"Usage Analytics","description":"# Usage Analytics

## Purpose
Track profile usage activity to give users visibility into their patterns and help identify underutilized or overutilized profiles.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool tracks detailed usage with BoltDB:
- Per-request logs (debugging)
- Per-account aggregates (dashboard)
- Automatic pruning (retention policy)

## Adaptation for caam
caam does not proxy requests, so we cannot track API usage directly. Instead, we track **caam activity**:
- Profile activations
- Login/refresh events
- Errors encountered
- Session durations

## What This Epic Delivers
1. SQLite database setup and schema
2. Activity logging API
3. Session duration tracking
4. Usage aggregation queries
5. CLI usage command
6. TUI usage panel (optional)

## Why SQLite (not BoltDB)
- Query flexibility (complex aggregations)
- Familiar tooling (sqlite3 CLI, DB browsers)
- Mature ecosystem
- CGO-free drivers available (modernc.org/sqlite)

## Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE activity_log (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    details TEXT,
    duration_seconds INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE profile_stats (
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    total_activations INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_active_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    last_activated DATETIME,
    last_error DATETIME,
    PRIMARY KEY (provider, profile_name)
);
```

## Event Types
- `activate` - Profile activated
- `deactivate` - Profile deactivated (implicit when switching)
- `login` - User logged in
- `refresh` - Token refreshed
- `error` - Error occurred
- `switch` - Switched from one profile to another

## Retention Policy
- Keep detailed logs for 30 days (configurable)
- Keep aggregates indefinitely
- Auto-prune on startup

## User Experience
```bash
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work
Session History for claude/work (last 7 days)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
```

## Dependencies
- None (parallel to health features)

## Future Enhancements
- Export to CSV/JSON
- Integration with provider usage APIs
- Cost tracking (if providers expose this)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:16:31.226242657-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:16:31.226242657-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-234","depends_on_id":"caam-3am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:26:48.554681767-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-3am","title":"Smart Profile Management (codex-pool inspired)","description":"# Smart Profile Management Epic

## Origin & Inspiration
This epic brings intelligence from [codex-pool](https://github.com/darvell/codex-pool) to caam. codex-pool is a sophisticated proxy that load-balances requests across multiple AI accounts with automatic failover, token refresh, and usage tracking.

**Key Insight**: While codex-pool answers "which account for THIS request?", caam answers "which account for my WORK SESSION?" The intelligence transfers, but the application differs.

## Goals
1. **Prevent auth failures** through proactive token refresh
2. **Surface profile health** so users can make informed choices
3. **Track activity** for visibility into usage patterns
4. **Reduce friction** with hot reload and project associations

## Non-Goals (for now)
- Proxy mode (request interception)
- Automatic profile switching mid-session
- Rate limit detection from API responses

## Success Metrics
- Zero unexpected auth failures due to token expiry
- Users can identify problematic profiles at a glance
- Usage patterns are visible via dashboard
- TUI updates without manual refresh

## Design Document
See `docs/SMART_PROFILE_MANAGEMENT.md` for detailed design rationale, architecture decisions, and risk analysis.

## Sub-Epics
1. **Profile Health Foundation** - Core infrastructure
2. **Proactive Token Refresh** - Automatic token maintenance
3. **Usage Analytics** - Activity tracking and dashboard
4. **Runtime Enhancements** - Hot reload, signals
5. **Project Context** - Directory-profile associations

## Technical Approach
- SQLite for persistent storage (usage.db)
- JSON for lightweight metadata (health.json)
- Provider-specific OAuth handlers
- fsnotify for file watching","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:15:04.594984626-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:15:04.594984626-05:00"}
